task_completion.description: Create a layer named 'PROFILE' with cyan color.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The layer list panel in LibreCAD shows a layer named 'PROFILE', ideally with a cyan color indicator.

task_completion.description: Create a layer named 'CUTOUTS' with yellow color.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The layer list panel in LibreCAD shows a layer named 'CUTOUTS', ideally with a yellow color indicator.

task_completion.description: Draw the airfoil profile as a closed loop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A closed, symmetric teardrop-like airfoil shape is visible on the drawing canvas.

task_completion.description: Draw three circular holes for the spars and lightening hole.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Three circles are drawn inside the airfoil along the horizontal centerline. From left to right: a medium circle (Main Spar), a large circle (Lightening Hole), and a small circle (Rear Spar).
The LibreCAD window title bar displays 'wing_rib.dxf' or a save dialog shows the file being saved with this name.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreCAD GUI to create the drawing.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the LibreCAD application interface being actively used, rather than just terminal commands or a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent actually created the geometry in LibreCAD.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show intermediate steps of drawing, importing coordinates, or configuring layers, rather than just opening a pre-existing completed file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The airfoil profile is perfectly symmetric, 200mm long, and has an 18% maximum thickness (36mm total height). Three holes must be present on the horizontal centerline: Main Spar at x=50 (10mm diameter), Lightening Hole at x=95 (24mm diameter), and Rear Spar at x=140 (6mm diameter). The 'PROFILE' layer should be cyan, and the 'CUTOUTS' layer should be yellow. The final output must be a valid DXF file named 'wing_rib.dxf'.
